title: Linear interpolation matches the left endpoint

source.citation: Alfio Quarteroni, Riccardo Sacco, and Fausto Saleri, Numerical Mathematics, 2nd ed., Springer, 2007, Ch. 8 (Polynomial Interpolation).
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: If the interpolation interval has nonzero length, the two-point linear interpolant evaluated at the left node returns the left datum.
informal.expected_conclusion: The linear interpolant matches y0 at x0.

declarations.name: linearInterp
declarations.statement: def linearInterp (x0 x1 y0 y1 x : Real) : Real := ((x1 - x) / (x1 - x0)) * y0 + ((x - x0) / (x1 - x0)) * y1

declarations.name: linearInterp_at_left
declarations.statement: theorem linearInterp_at_left (x0 x1 y0 y1 : Real) (h : x1 - x0 ≠ 0) : linearInterp x0 x1 y0 y1 x0 = y0

tasks.input: Translate the interpolation theorem `linearInterp_at_left` into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem linearInterp_at_left (x0 x1 y0 y1 : Real) (h : x1 - x0 ≠ 0) : linearInterp x0 x1 y0 y1 x0 = y0

tasks.input: Identify the main algebraic or order lemma needed for the interpolation proof.
